plan: Trim SPY to 20% to meet concentration limits and reduce overall broad index exposure slightly.
Sell SOXL and SPAL entirely to eliminate all leveraged ETF exposure, aligning with capital preservation.
Reduce SMH to 10% due to its downtrend and to lower sector concentration.
Increase QQQI to 25% for more income and broad market exposure.
Initiate a new position in XLV at 5% for diversification into a top-quality, less volatile sector.
conservative.read: This conservative plan aims for capital preservation with a target of ~1.5%/month. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration, favoring stable income and broad market exposure. Expect lower potential drawdowns but also more modest growth.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to adhere to concentration limits and manage broad index exposure.
Sell SOXL entirely as it is below stop, and reduce SPAL to 3% to keep leveraged ETFs small.
Reduce SMH to 10% to manage exposure to a downtrending sector.
Increase CRWD to 15% to lean into a performing AI/Software holding.
Initiate a new position in LRCX at 7% to add a strong, high-scoring semiconductor name.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets ~2.5%/month, focusing on risk-first growth. It reduces overall leverage and concentration while blending broad index with quality tech. Expect moderate growth potential with managed drawdowns.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to comply with concentration limits, freeing capital for higher growth.
Sell SOXL entirely as it is below its stop, reallocating capital to stronger setups.
Reduce SMH to 5% due to its downtrend, focusing on uptrending opportunities.
Increase CRWD to 20% to press a high-performing AI/Software holding.
Initiate new positions in LRCX (10%) and ASML (5%) to add high-scoring semiconductor exposure.
Increase SPAL to 10% to utilize leveraged ETFs for enhanced growth potential, within mandate.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets ~4%/month, aiming for stretched growth. It utilizes leverage and concentrates in high-scoring setups, which amplifies both upside and drawdown potential. Be aware that leveraged ETFs decay daily, and this approach can lead to significant losses quickly if not managed with strict stop discipline.

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce broad index exposure and free capital for income-focused assets.
Sell SOXL and SPAL entirely to eliminate all leveraged ETF exposure, aligning with income goals.
Sell SMH to exit a high-beta, downtrending sector, further de-risking the portfolio.
Reduce CRWD to 5% to minimize exposure to high-beta single names.
Increase QQQI to 40% to maximize the portfolio's focus on steady monthly distributions.
income.read: This income plan targets ~1%/month, prioritizing steady monthly distributions. It significantly reduces leverage and high-beta single names, rotating into income-generating ETFs. While aiming for low drawdown, distributions are variable, and NAV can still fall in a selloff, so it's not a savings account.
